data.title: Cryptocurrency Market Sentiment Cools as Fear Index Drops
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, the cryptocurrency fear and greed index has dropped to 45 today, down from 57 yesterday, indicating a rapid cooling of market sentiment into the 'fear' range. This marks the first time since April 22 that the index has fallen into this category.

The fear and greed index, which ranges from 0 to 100, is calculated based on several factors: volatility (25%), market trading volume (25%), social media activity (15%), market surveys (15%), Bitcoin's market dominance (10%), and Google search trends (10%).

data.title: OpenAI Expands Educational Platform to India with New Academy
data.description: According to PANews, OpenAI has announced a collaboration with the Indian government’s IndiaAI Mission to establish OpenAI Academy India, marking its first international expansion of the educational platform. The academy aims to provide AI skills training to students, civil servants, and entrepreneurs through a combination of online and offline methods. Additionally, it will offer up to $100,000 in API support, host hackathons, and conduct expert seminars to foster AI talent development and local innovation in India.

data.title: How a Startup Could Avoid SEC Scrutiny With Strong Compliance Strategies
data.description:
Do you know that one misstep from a Web 3. 0 founder could bring the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) knocking at the doorstep of the company unannounced? This has been the fate of reputable projects like Ripple, Block. one and so on. Their regulatory missteps have turned into multi-million-dollar headaches for years now
source: https://dailyhodl.com/2025/06/05/how-a-startup-could-avoid-sec-scrutiny-with-strong-compliance-strategies/

data.title: Hong Kong-listed company China Renaissance Capital disclosed that it invested in Circle in 2018 and continues to be optimistic about the development of blockchain technology
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Hong Kong-listed company Huaxing Capital disclosed that its Huaxing New Economy Fund had invested in Circle in 2018. The company will continue to be optimistic about the development of blockchain technology and actively explore the layout in the field of Web3.0 and cryptocurrency assets. Circle Internet Group was listed on the New York Stock Exchange on Thursday, with a peak of $103.75, a surge of 2.35 times from the IPO price of $31, and closed at $83.23, still up 1.68 times. After the market, it rose another 5.38% to $87.71, and its market value after listing reached $6.9 billion.

data.title: Circle Internet Group's Stock Surges on NYSE Debut
data.description:
According to Odaily, Hong Kong-listed company Huaxing Capital has revealed that its Huaxing New Economy Fund invested in Circle in 2018. The company remains optimistic about the development of blockchain technology and is actively exploring opportunities in the Web3.0 and cryptocurrency asset sectors.

Circle Internet Group made its debut on the New York Stock Exchange on Thursday, with its stock price reaching a high of $103.75, a 2.35-fold increase from its initial public offering price of $31. The stock closed at $83.23, marking a 1.68-fold rise. In after-hours trading, the stock further increased by 5.38%, reaching $87.71, bringing the company's market capitalization to $6.9 billion.

data.title: Sky Union repurchased 4.33 million SKY in the past two days, equivalent to about 330,000 US dollars
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to the monitoring of chain data analyst Yu Jin, the address of Rune Christensen, the co-founder of Sky (formerly MakerDAO), has repurchased 4.33 million SKY (about 330,000 US dollars) in the past 2 days using staking rewards.
Sky launched the staking SKY reward USDS a few days ago. The Rune address has staked 3.16 billion SKY (about 229 million US dollars), and currently can get about 100,000 USDS per day. He received two staking rewards on the 4th and early this morning, a total of 330,000 USDS, and then repurchased 4.33 million SKY at a price of 0.076 US dollars.

data.title: Hyperliquid’s 50x leveraged BTC short position turned from loss to profit, with a floating profit of over $600,000
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: According to hypurrscan data, as the price of BTC fell this morning, Hyperliquid's 50x leverage whale turned its previous BTC short position into a profit, with a current floating profit of over $600,000. Its current position is 394 BTC short positions, with a total value of over $40 million, an average opening price of $103,772, and a liquidation price of $104,230.

data.title: Market Sentiment Turns Bearish as Funding Rates Decline
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, data from Coinglass indicates that the current funding rates on major centralized and decentralized exchanges suggest a bearish market sentiment. The funding rates for key cryptocurrencies are detailed in the accompanying data.

Funding rates are fees set by cryptocurrency trading platforms to maintain the balance between contract prices and the prices of underlying assets, typically applied to perpetual contracts. This mechanism facilitates the exchange of funds between long and short traders, without the platform charging this fee, to adjust the cost or profit of holding contracts, ensuring that contract prices remain close to the asset prices.

A funding rate of 0.01% is considered the baseline rate. When the funding rate exceeds 0.01%, it indicates a generally bullish market sentiment. Conversely, when the funding rate falls below 0.005%, it reflects a bearish market outlook.

data.title: James Wynn confessed: He was afraid of being laughed at for "making $100 million but couldn't keep it", and he became a gambler because of greed
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News James Wynn posted on the X platform that he had just started to participate in perpetual contract trading in March this year. He had never traded seriously before, and at most he speculated on MEME coins (previously he was famous for discovering PEPE at a market value of 600,000 and making 8-digit profits). He once rolled over 3 million US dollars to 100 million in just one month, and then lost it all in one week on HyperLiquid. At that time, he was just playing around, but the on-chain data was public, and hundreds of thousands of people watched his account soar and plummet, so he simply let himself go. Later, things gradually got out of control. He understood that this was essentially gambling. He wanted to recover his losses, but he was also afraid of being laughed at for "making 100 million and not being able to keep it", so he got deeper and deeper. The numbers jumping on the screen became a virtual game, and greed completely prevailed.

data.title: SlowMist: Lazarus hackers are using a new stealing Trojan, OtterCookie, to target cryptographic practitioners
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News SlowMist Security Team issued a warning that the North Korean Lazarus hacker group is using a new stealing Trojan called OtterCookie to launch targeted attacks against cryptocurrency and financial practitioners.
The attack methods include faking high-paying job interviews/investor negotiations, using deep fake videos to impersonate recruiters, and disguising malware as "programming test questions" or "system update packages."
The stolen targets include login credentials saved by browsers, passwords and digital certificates in macOS keychains, and encrypted wallet information and private keys.
SlowMist recommends being vigilant about unsolicited job/investment invitations, requiring multiple verifications for remote interviews, and never running executable files of unknown origin, especially so-called "technical test questions" or "update patches", strengthening terminal protection (EDR), deploying antivirus software and regularly troubleshooting abnormal processes.

data.title: North Korean Hackers Target Cryptocurrency Sector with New Malware
data.description:
According to Odaily, the cybersecurity team at SlowMist has issued a warning about the North Korean hacker group Lazarus, which is employing a new type of malware named OtterCookie to launch targeted attacks on cryptocurrency and financial professionals. The attack strategies include fabricating high-paying job interviews or investor meetings, using deepfake videos to impersonate recruiters, and disguising malware as 'programming tests' or 'system update packages.' The malware aims to steal credentials saved in browsers, passwords and digital certificates from macOS keychains, as well as information and private keys from cryptocurrency wallets.

SlowMist advises maintaining vigilance against unsolicited job offers or investment invitations, ensuring multiple verifications for remote interviews, and avoiding running unknown executable files, particularly those labeled as 'technical tests' or 'update patches.' They recommend strengthening endpoint protection (EDR), deploying antivirus software, and regularly checking for abnormal processes.
